title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
summary: Nupur Sharma argues that simply expanding context windows does not solve the problem of agents neglecting middle sections (the U curve effect). She advocates for iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction as solutions, and proposes an 80/20 split of high-reasoning and lightweight deterministic models to manage token efficiency in agent orchestration.
writeup: Nupur Sharma of Qodo argues that expanding context windows does not by itself improve agent performance, citing a U-curve pattern in which agents attend to the start and end of context while neglecting the middle. She also describes an orchestration paradox where tokens are spent on planning rather than execution. The talk proposes concrete remedies: iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and a hybrid 80/20 split between high-reasoning and deterministic models.
